message:
fix(abi): handle EINTR in stdio write loops and add proper retry logic

All flush/write paths in fclose, fputc, fputs, fwrite, and fseek previously
treated any non-positive return from sys_write_fd (rc <= 0) as a terminal
failure. This is incorrect on POSIX: a write interrupted by a signal returns
-1 with errno == EINTR and should be retried rather than aborting the
operation.

Split the error check into rc < 0 (inspect errno, retry on EINTR, break
otherwise) and rc == 0 (true zero-length write, break as before). The printf
and dprintf fallback paths, which previously issued a single sys_write_fd
call and compared the result to total_len, are now wrapped in proper write
loops with the same EINTR-aware retry logic so that partial writes and
signal interrupts are handled correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
